steps.title: Distribute 10 and rewrite the expression
steps.content:
Apply the distributive property to $10 \times (10y^2 - 110)$:
$$10 \times (10y^2 - 110) = 100y^2 - 1100$$

Substitute back into the original expression:
$$(7532 + 100y^2) + (100y^2 - 1100)$$

steps.title: Combine like terms and calculate a + b
steps.content:
Group the $y^2$ terms: $100y^2 + 100y^2 = 200y^2$
Group the constants: $7532 - 1100 = 6432$
The expression in the form $ay^2 + b$ is: $$200y^2 + 6432$$ Identify the coefficients: $a = 200$ and $b = 6432$. Therefore: $$a + b = 200 + 6432 = 6632$$
